evidence_summary.fc_a_011: text02 p1 gives the Shenzhen Financial Leasing issuer and RMB 0.5bn cap; text07 p1 gives Shenzhen Stock Exchange and identifies a restructuring report.
evidence_summary.fc_a_002: text01 p1 and text14 p1/p4 give AAA issuers and RMB 1bn/RMB 2bn caps; '-' means no issue rating, not a negative rating.
evidence_summary.fc_a_007: text02 p1 and text14 p1 distinguish the leasing/securities issuers, caps and ratings; text02 p192 and text14 p171 enumerate default provisions.
evidence_summary.fc_a_012: text03 p11 gives the 2031-04-23 payment date; text13 p55/p57 gives rising profit and declining leverage; text03 p167 contains the 150% default-related formula. The labeler recorded a terminology caveat but selected ACD under the question's ordinary wording.

evidence_summary.fin_a_017: CATL 2024 annual report p25 gives a roughly RMB 29.2bn financing-cash-flow decrease; CSCEC p10/p88 gives RMB 11.218bn dividends and 24.29% of attributable profit.
evidence_summary.fin_a_014: CATL 2025 p11/p59 gives 17.04% revenue growth and the 50%-of-profit dividend plan; BYD 2025 p32/p39 gives R&D above 5% and p40 gives negative operating-cash-flow growth.
evidence_summary.fin_a_009: China Mobile 2025 p8 gives RMB 1.050187tn revenue and positive operating cash flow; BYD 2024 p34 gives 6.97% R&D intensity and positive operating cash flow.
evidence_summary.fin_a_007: BYD 2024 p34 gives 6.97% R&D and -21.37% operating-cash-flow growth; CATL 2025 p58 and p30 give RMB 69.57 per ten shares and 37.35% operating-cash-flow growth.

evidence_summary.reg_a_016: CSRC governance rules articles 10/19 support A/B; csrc_0271 supports D and contradicts C's claimed penalty basis.
evidence_summary.reg_a_011: csrc_0038_att1 article 12, customer-due-diligence articles 13/32/44 support A/C/D and refute B.
evidence_summary.reg_a_009: PBOC data-security articles 22/42/52 support A/B/D; customer-due-diligence article 44 requires ten years, refuting C.
evidence_summary.reg_a_020: csrc_0037_att1 article 12 supports A/B; csrc_0027_att1 article 35 gives the effective date supporting D.
evidence_summary.res_a_009: pack2_text01 pp3-4 supports A/C; pack2_text02 p22 supports B and says the 3384 rules automate, not manually perform, parsing.
evidence_summary.res_a_012: pack2_text09 pp1/6/25 and pack2_text02 p22 support A/B/C; the 3384 rules are for automatic parsing, refuting D.
evidence_summary.res_a_020: pack2_text09 pp1/6, pack2_text04 pp2/90 support A/B/D; pack2_text09 p25 attributes the FY27 forecast to Broadcom, refuting C.
